directive_work.directive_title: Design a new readability-focused agent named 'sloppytoppy'
deliverables: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-039-sloppytoppy-readability-agent.json (96KB; full PROP with all 8 task deliverables addressed, 5-surface hand-calibration sample, embedded sloppytoppy.md prompt draft, embedded sloppytoppy-format-spec.md draft, 7 counter-arguments including 2 tinker-added ones, 8 open questions for operator)
directive_work.directive_status_action: Marked DIRECTIVE-20260516-001 status=completed (additive edit per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE exception), completed_at=2026-05-16T05:50:00Z, completed_by_run=tinker-2026-05-16T05-40, prop_id_authored=PROP-039, amendment_file_authored=monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-039-sloppytoppy-readability-agent.json.
directive_work.design_headline: Sloppytoppy is a new daily-cadence dual-mode agent: Phase 1 scoring (Sonnet) audits readability across ~420 surfaces (305 WIN-field + 115 section-<details>); Phase 2 (conditional) rewrites (Opus) low-scored surfaces with three-mechanism gating (acceptable-floor 7.5, minimum-improvement-delta 1.5, cooldown 14 days) and kernel-of-truth preservation check. Rewrites enter pipeline as analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN with authored_by='sloppytoppy', review_class='verification', class_hint='readability-rewrite'. Curmudgeon-verify (PROP-038) handles post-rewrite review cheaply. Punt mechanism (monitor/sloppytoppy/punts/) routes integrity-uncertain rewrites to analyst.
directive_work.operator_amendment_2026_05_16_addressed: task_1 Q3b three-mechanism rewrite threshold (acceptable-floor + minimum-improvement-delta + cooldown window) fully designed in scoring_rubric.rewrite_threshold_mechanisms section. Predicted-improvement gating method recommended: Option ii (draft-then-score-then-decide), with rationale.
key_design_choices_summary: Two-axis rubric (length 1-10 + understandability 1-10, composite 0.4×L + 0.6×U). Understandability dominates because verbose-but-clear is acceptable; short-but-obscure is the worst pattern.
Per-field WIN scoring (4 always + detail_extra when present) + per-<details>-block section scoring → ~420 surfaces. Source-of-truth scoring (wins.json + sections.json), not generated HTML.
Sonnet for scoring (rule-compliance) + Opus for rewriting (judgment-heavy) — mirrors PROP-034 model-class pattern.

Math-dense flag (sloppytoppy/math-dense-surfaces.json) raises length thresholds 50% and relaxes jargon/concept-stacking deductions for surfaces where the math IS the argument. Seed list: WIN-001/038/052/062 detail_evidence + parts of part4.
Phase 1 = scoring-only 7 days. Phase 2 conditional rewrites 14 days probation. Phase 3 long-tail format-adoption by analyst/curmudgeon (separate PROP).
Daily 03:30 UTC cadence (off-peak; after analyst/curmudgeon, before workspace-sync).

5-surface hand-calibration sample included (WIN-001/019/038/052/060 tldr_verdicts + WIN-038 detail_evidence). Phase 1 exit criteria require operator validation against this sample plus a top-10/bottom-10 spot-check.

7 counter-arguments addressed (5 anticipated by directive + 2 tinker-added). Highest-importance counter (#6): central failure mode is rubric mis-scoring kernel-of-truth content. Defense-in-depth: math-dense flag, kernel-of-truth semantic-distance check, punt mechanism, curmudgeon post-commit review.

findings.summary: DIRECTIVE-20260516-001 executed end-to-end in one run. PROP-039 authored with all 8 task deliverables, embedded prompt + format-spec drafts, and 5-surface hand-calibration sample. Directive marked completed.
evidence: PROP-039 contains: scoring rubric (two-axis with explicit deduction tables); section breakdown unit design (305 WIN-field + 115 section-block surfaces); internal format spec (per-surface templates); dispatcher + run modes (Mode 1 scoring + Mode 2 rewriting with three-mechanism gating); phased implementation plan (Phase 1/2/3 with exit + rollback criteria); scheduled task design (daily 03:30 UTC); counter-argument section (7 args); cost analysis (~$22.50/mo Phase 1, ~$150/mo Phase 2); 8 open questions for operator

findings.background: PROP-016 Mech A is working as designed — decider correctly identifies patches it cannot self-apply (NEVER_PUSH-file targets) and strands them with full context for operator action. The stranding itself is not a pipeline failure. The 24h-aging just signals operator hasn't actioned them yet.
